topic: Detecting AI-generated commits and code contributions in git history

sources.title: Fingerprinting AI Coding Agents on GitHub (arXiv:2601.17406)
keyInsights: 97.2% F1-score for multi-class agent identification using 41 features
Multiline commit ratio is dominant feature at 44.7% importance
Codex multiline patterns 67.5%, Claude conditional density 27.2%
33,580 PRs from 5 agents analyzed with XGBoost
Claude Code has lowest recall at 57% due to small sample size

sources.title: AIDev: Studying AI Coding Agents on GitHub
keyInsights: 932,791 agent-authored PRs from 5 agents across 116,211 repos
88,576 commits and 711,923 file-level diffs analyzed
Agentic PRs differ most in commit structure and file breadth
Dataset available on Hugging Face and Zenodo

sources.title: LLM-generated Code Stylometry for Authorship Attribution
keyInsights: CodeT5-Authorship achieves 97.56% accuracy for binary LLM attribution
Comment style is dominant signal (2-3pp impact when removed)
Claude favors longer identifiers, ChatGPT shorter names
95.40% multi-class accuracy across 5 LLMs
Limited to C language; generalization unknown

sources.title: Agent Trace Specification (by Cursor)
keyInsights: JSON-based open spec for AI code attribution at file/line level
Contributor types: human, ai, mixed, unknown
Content hashing via murmur3 for tracking code across moves
Partners include Cline, Cognition, Jules, Vercel, Cloudflare
Storage-agnostic: files, git notes, database

sources.title: Git AI - How It Works (vendor-agnostic tracking)
keyInsights: Uses git notes (--ref=ai) for metadata storage
Pre/post-edit checkpoints capture AI vs human changes
Authorship logs map line ranges to agent sessions
Preserves attribution through rebases and squashes
Requires proactive agent integration

sources.title: Our Coding Agent Commits Deserve Better Than Co-Authored-By
keyInsights: Co-Authored-By was designed for humans, not AI metadata
Proposes Coding-Agent and Model trailers as alternatives
AI-assistant trailer captures tool+model in single field
Format changes between Claude Code releases cause inconsistencies
Same model produces different output across different agent tools

sources.title: Aider Git Integration Documentation
keyInsights: Co-authored-by: aider (model) <noreply@aider.chat> format
(aider) appended to git author/committer name
aider: prefix for commit messages configurable
Conventional commits format by default
All attribution options independently configurable

sources.title: About GitHub Copilot Coding Agent - GitHub Docs
keyInsights: Branch prefix copilot/ is hardcoded security restriction
One PR per assigned issue
Co-authored by requesting developer
Cannot push to default branches
Commits authored as GitHub Copilot bot

sources.title: Copilot Coding Agent Better Branch Names - GitHub Changelog
keyInsights: Evolved from random UUIDs to descriptive copilot/ branch names
Example: copilot/add-theme-switcher instead of copilot/fix-bdaf7923

sources.title: Claude Code Settings - Attribution Configuration
keyInsights: Attribution setting controls commit and PR attribution separately
Default: Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Generated with Claude Code text in commit body
Can be fully disabled via settings

sources.title: Claude Code - Allow disabling attribution in git commits
keyInsights: Users request ability to disable Claude attribution
includeCoAuthoredBy setting sometimes not respected
Format inconsistencies between releases

sources.title: Cursor Co-Author Added Without Consent
keyInsights: Cursor auto-adds Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Cannot be turned off by users
Users develop workaround scripts to filter attribution

sources.title: Cursor Background Agents Documentation
keyInsights: Background Agent creates cursor/ prefixed branches
Branch naming not customizable
May ignore commit message rules set in .cursor/rules

sources.title: Does OpenAI Codex Add Default Commit-Level Metadata?
keyInsights: Codex did not add commit attribution by default initially
command_attribution config added later
No hidden signals or API metadata in git history
Configurable via ~/.codex/config.toml

sources.title: Codex: Prompt-based co-author attribution with config override
keyInsights: Hook-based co-author attribution via codex_git_commit
command_attribution config supports custom labels
Can be disabled with empty string
Treats override as verbatim trailer text

sources.title: Devin Review - Devin Documentation
keyInsights: Commits made as Devin bot with bot@devin.ai email
Custom PR template support
GitHub App integration as devin-ai-integration[bot]

sources.title: Google Jules Documentation
keyInsights: Three authorship modes: Jules sole, co-authored, user sole
Jules listed as commit author by default
PR author is the user, not Jules
Supports Agent Trace specification

sources.title: VibeDetect - AI Code Detection Tool
keyInsights: Weighted scoring system combining multiple analyzer categories
Signals: template similarity, dependency selection, naming patterns
Score 0-100 mapped to human-readable verdicts
Deterministic heuristics + optional LLM analysis

sources.title: IsVibeCoded - Detect AI-generated code in GitHub repos
keyInsights: Analyzes 5 features: naming, repetition, comments, boilerplate, creativity
Provides confidence score (0-100)
Entertainment/insight only, not for hiring/integrity
AI: verbose names, consistent formatting, formal docs

sources.title: AI Usage Measurement Framework
keyInsights: Pattern matching on commit messages for tool signatures
Confidence levels: High (0.9), Medium (0.7-0.85), Low (0.3-0.6)
Supports 10 AI tools via string matching
Privacy-friendly local analysis only

sources.title: SonarQube Autodetect AI Code
keyInsights: Detects Copilot usage via GitHub App API (user activity, not code patterns)
DEPRECATED in SonarQube Server 2026.1
Limited to GitHub Copilot only
No code-level analysis

sources.title: Spotting LLMs With Binoculars: Zero-Shot Detection (ICML 2024)
keyInsights: 90%+ detection rate at 0.01% false positive rate
Zero-shot, domain-agnostic approach
Contrasts two related LLMs (observer + performer)
Outperforms GPTZero, Ghostbuster, DetectGPT, Fast-DetectGPT
Not specifically validated for code

sources.title: Detecting AI-Generated Code Using Perplexity (AAAI 2024)
keyInsights: Targeted masking perturbation on high-perplexity code areas
AUC 87.81% for >50 LOC, drops to 69.75% for <20 LOC
AI code has more uniform perplexity distribution
Line-level granularity possible via per-token perplexity

sources.title: GPTZero Technology - AI Detection Methodology
keyInsights: 7-component detection model for AI-generated text
Code detection AUC only 0.64 (poor to fair)
Examines perplexity, burstiness, and style
Not effective for code compared to prose

sources.title: Code Fingerprints: Disentangled Attribution of LLM-Generated Code
keyInsights: Recent (2026) work on LLM code attribution
Disentangled approach separating style from content

sources.title: How to Use Git with Claude Code: Co-Authored-By Attribution
keyInsights: Default format: Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
\n\n separator required by git trailers format
Attribution setting controls commit and PR separately

sources.title: Prevent Copilot Coding Agent from being main author of commits
keyInsights: Squash-and-merge sets Copilot as primary author
No official GitHub solution for author override
Workarounds via Co-authored-by trailers ineffective

sources.title: How Git Worktrees Changed My AI Agent Workflow
keyInsights: Worktrees enable parallel AI agent workflows
Each agent gets isolated working directory
Branch-per-task pattern for agent work
Prevents context contamination between tasks

sources.title: Why Squash Merge No Longer Works in the Age of AI
keyInsights: AI agents produce PRs with 50+ tests and 1000+ lines
Squash merge obscures AI authorship attribution
Merge commits recommended to preserve AI attribution

sources.title: Do You Attribute AI-Assisted Commits with Co-Authors?
keyInsights: Best practice: attribute AI commits with Co-authored-by trailers
Standard format for multiple AI tools
Team governance recommendations

sources.title: Why Perplexity and Burstiness Fail to Detect AI
keyInsights: Newer LLMs produce higher-entropy, more varied text
Signature patterns less pronounced in 2025+ models
Human writing can exhibit AI-like patterns

sources.title: Understanding False Positives in AI Code Detection
keyInsights: Good developers write clean code matching AI patterns
Version history tells clearer story than static analysis
Code in popular stacks more likely misclassified
Behavior-based validation better than detection heuristics

sources.title: How to Detect AI-Generated Code in Your Software Projects
keyInsights: Statistical analysis, ML models, syntax evaluation approaches
Pattern comparison against known AI-generated code databases
CI pipeline integration for automated detection

sources.title: Windsurf AI Commit Messages Documentation
keyInsights: Single-click AI commit message generation
No Co-Authored-By trailer or attribution
Analyzes code changes for message content
One of hardest tools to detect

sources.title: Cline Git Integration - Generate Commit Message
keyInsights: Generates commit messages via getWorkingState() and git diff
No attribution trailer or author modification
Uses VSCode Git extension API

sources.title: Tabnine Provenance and Attribution
keyInsights: Provenance tracing against GitHub public code
License-aware attribution with source references
Postgres-based signature database
CI/CD integration for license compliance

sources.title: git-ai: Git Extension for Tracking AI-Generated Code
keyInsights: Vendor-agnostic, zero-configuration CLI
Checkpoints stored in .git/ai/
Authorship logs attached via git notes
Heuristic-free approach: agents explicitly mark their code

sources.title: AI Usage Measurement Framework (GitHub)
keyInsights: Commit message pattern matching for 10 AI tools
Confidence scoring: High/Medium/Low
Also analyzes Agents.md files
Open source, privacy-friendly

sources.title: Identifying Unusual Commits on GitHub (CMU Research)
keyInsights: Commit time-of-day analysis for anomaly detection
Per-developer timing baselines for unusual detection
Anomaly scores based on commit frequency patterns
Applicable to detecting autonomous agent timing

sources.title: Commit Messages Aren't Just For Humans (Cline)
keyInsights: AI agents parse commit messages for context signals
Commit quality directly impacts AI code generation quality
Vague commits force agent to guess intent

sources.title: Attribute Git Commits to AI Agents
keyInsights: Recommended: --author='AI <agent@example.com>' for agent commits
Git distinguishes author vs committer for attribution
Best practices for team AI commit governance
